evals.name: design-direction-choice
evals.prompt: I'm building a pricing page. Should I use cards side-by-side or a comparison table? The page has 3 plans with 8 features each.

assertions.text: Recommends cards for visual hierarchy (each plan is a focal point)
Mentions that cards work better on mobile (stack vertically)
Suggests highlighting the recommended plan with primary color or elevation
Notes the table approach is acceptable on desktop but problematic on mobile
References spacing rhythm (consistent padding within cards)
Recommends a single prominent CTA per card

evals.name: accessibility-audit
evals.prompt: I have a button that's just an icon (trash can) for deleting items. The icon is 20x20px and uses the error color. Is this accessible?

assertions.text: Identifies missing aria-label as critical issue
Notes 20x20px is below the 44x44px minimum touch target
Mentions aria-label must use t() for i18n
Recommends expanding the clickable area with padding
Notes color alone is insufficient — needs text or aria context
Suggests adding a confirmation step for destructive actions

evals.name: responsive-layout-decision
evals.prompt: I have a dashboard with 4 metric cards, a chart, and a data table. How should this lay out across breakpoints?

assertions.text: Recommends mobile-first: stacked single column on xs
Suggests 2x2 grid for metric cards on sm/md, 4-across on lg+
Mentions chart should be full-width on all breakpoints
Recommends card view for data table on mobile, full table on lg+
References the breakpoint values from the design system
Discusses content priority (metrics > chart > table)

evals.name: micro-interaction-design
evals.prompt: I want to add feedback when a user successfully submits a form. What's the best approach?

assertions.text: Recommends v-snackbar with success color for transient confirmation
Mentions appropriate duration (around 3000ms)
Suggests button loading state during submission
Notes the button should show disabled/loading while request is in flight
Mentions respecting prefers-reduced-motion
May suggest optimistic UI pattern if action is quick

evals.name: visual-hierarchy-review
evals.prompt: My page has three buttons: 'Save Draft', 'Preview', and 'Publish'. They're all using the primary blue color with filled variant. Is this correct?

assertions.text: Identifies the problem: all buttons competing for attention equally
Recommends only 'Publish' (primary action) uses filled primary variant
Suggests 'Preview' as outlined or text variant (secondary action)
Suggests 'Save Draft' as text variant or neutral color (tertiary action)
References size > color > weight > position hierarchy principle
May mention positioning: primary action rightmost in button group

evals.name: dark-mode-color-semantics
evals.prompt: I'm adding a status badge component that uses green for active, red for inactive, and yellow for pending. How should this work in dark mode?

assertions.text: Notes colors must maintain semantic meaning across modes
Recommends adjusting saturation/brightness for dark backgrounds (lighter tints)
Mentions contrast requirements still apply (3:1 minimum for UI components)
Suggests pairing color with text label or icon (color not sole indicator)
References project's SCSS variables rather than hardcoded hex values
May recommend tonal variant for badges (Vuetify's default)
